skill.name: homeassistant-config
skill.description: Create, validate, and improve Home Assistant YAML configurations for automations, scripts, templates, blueprints, and Lovelace dashboards.
skill.summary: Build and check Home Assistant configuration files with focused YAML guidance and local validation tools.
skill.author: ESJavadex

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported critical and high-risk patterns, but manual review found they are mostly false positives from Home Assistant YAML, Markdown code fences, and placeholder examples. The included Python tools read user-supplied local YAML files for validation and duplicate analysis, with no confirmed command execution, outbound network calls, credential exfiltration, or prompt injection. Publishable with a low-risk filesystem warning because the scripts inspect local configuration files.

low_findings.title: Local Configuration File Reading
low_findings.description: The helper scripts accept user-supplied file or directory paths and read YAML or Lovelace configuration files for validation. This is expected for the skill, but users should run it only on intended Home Assistant configuration directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts clearly read local paths passed by the user. Review found no writes, deletion, command execution, or network transfer tied to those reads.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Secrets in Examples
low_findings.description: Example files include placeholder API keys, passwords, database URLs, and camera URLs to show Home Assistant secrets structure. The values are not real credentials, but users should avoid copying real secrets into version-controlled files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged values are visibly placeholders and the documentation warns not to commit secrets. The remaining risk is user misuse, not malicious skill behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto Alerts Are Non-Security Uses
low_findings.description: The confirmed MD5 use computes stable hashes for duplicate automation comparison, not password storage, signing, or encryption. Other weak-crypto alerts are YAML identifiers, modes, and Home Assistant terms misclassified by pattern matching.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only actual hash call is used for non-adversarial duplicate detection. The sampled YAML alerts are ordinary Home Assistant fields, not cryptographic operations.

content.value_statement: Home Assistant YAML can break when syntax, entity names, or dashboard structure drift. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create, review, and validate local configuration files.
actual_capabilities: Create Home Assistant automations using modern trigger, condition, and action structure.
Generate reusable scripts, scenes, template sensors, and blueprint patterns.
Validate YAML syntax and common Home Assistant structure with local Python scripts.
Find duplicate or similar automations and scripts across local YAML files.
Review Lovelace dashboard files for views, cards, actions, and entity references.
Provide examples for secrets organization, includes, dashboard cards, and maintenance practices.
limitations: It does not connect to a running Home Assistant instance or verify live entity states.
Validation scripts require Python 3 and PyYAML in the local environment.
Generated YAML still needs testing in Home Assistant before production use.
Custom Lovelace cards must be installed separately through Home Assistant or HACS.

use_cases.title: Create reliable starter automations
use_cases.description: Build motion lighting, presence routines, and notifications with correct YAML structure.

use_cases.title: Clean up existing configuration
use_cases.description: Check local files for syntax problems, duplicate rules, and inconsistent entity references.

use_cases.title: Design Lovelace views
use_cases.description: Plan dashboards with cards, sections, navigation, and custom card usage notes.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Automation
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a simple motion light rule.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Home Assistant automation that turns on hallway lights after sunset when motion is detected, then turns them off after five minutes.

prompt_templates.title: Script Refactor
prompt_templates.scenario: Convert repeated actions into a script.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my repeated light and climate actions, then suggest a reusable Home Assistant script with clear fields and safe defaults.

prompt_templates.title: Dashboard Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Lovelace dashboard for invalid card structure, missing entity references, and layout improvements for mobile screens.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Blueprint
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Home Assistant blueprint for adaptive motion lighting with configurable sensors, target lights, timeout, brightness, and night mode behavior.

output_examples.input: Create a night hallway motion automation.
output_examples.output: A complete automation plan with a motion trigger, sun condition, restart mode, target hallway light, brightness setting, and delayed turn-off behavior.

output_examples.input: Check my Lovelace dashboard for issues.
output_examples.output: A review summary listing invalid card types, missing navigation fields, unknown custom cards, and entity references that need verification.

output_examples.input: Find duplicate automations in my configuration folder.
output_examples.output: A local analysis summary showing files scanned, exact duplicate groups, similar automation candidates, and trigger conflicts to review.

best_practices: Keep secrets in Home Assistant secrets files and exclude real values from version control.
Run validation scripts on a copy or controlled configuration directory before applying changes.
Use clear automation IDs, descriptions, and modes so behavior remains predictable.
anti_patterns: Pasting real passwords, API keys, camera URLs, or database URLs into shared examples.
Assuming generated YAML works without reloading and testing it in Home Assistant.
Mixing old and new automation syntax in the same file without a deliberate migration plan.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to Home Assistant?
faq.answer: No. It creates and validates configuration content locally. It does not call the Home Assistant API.

faq.question: What files can it help with?
faq.answer: It targets YAML configuration, automations, scripts, templates, blueprints, secrets examples, and Lovelace dashboard files.

faq.question: Are the validation scripts safe to run?
faq.answer: They read local files and print analysis results. Run them only against directories you intend to inspect.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Can it validate custom cards?
faq.answer: It can flag known and unknown custom Lovelace card types, but it cannot confirm your HACS installation state.

faq.question: Should I publish real secrets with the examples?
faq.answer: No. Use placeholders in examples and keep real secrets out of shared repositories.
